items.summary: Update the backend `generateHowToUseGuide` so the returned `howToUse` text is actually derived from the pickup line content and does not return the same generic guide for every line.

Implement real `detectLineType` logic in `backend/main.mo` so that it classifies pickup lines into at least several distinct categories (e.g., bold/clever/subtle/playful/cheesy/rhyming/classic) based on simple deterministic heuristics over the text (keywords, punctuation, rhyme-like endings, explicitness, etc.), and use the detected type to select different context/delivery/timing/follow-up guidance.

Stop repeating the pickup line text inside the backend-generated `howToUse` guide text (the frontend already displays the pickup line under “The Line”). The `howToUse` string returned by `getLineWithGuide` must contain only guidance content (e.g., Context, How to Use, Timing, Follow-up), not the original pickup line text duplicated at the top.
Ensure the generated guide never renders a blank/empty “Context:” section; if no specific context can be inferred, return a non-empty generic context sentence in English.
